Grant source lib.rs:3024-3051 load_catalog_grants: `select table_schema, table_name, grantee, privilege, grantable, hierarchy from all_tab_privs ...` — only `grantee`, no user/role discriminator column (Oracle's ALL_TAB_PRIVS has none).
`rg 'Grantee::Role\(' crates/` => only crates/plsql-catalog/src/synthetic.rs:424/430/436 (test/demo builder) and the two consumers in plsql-privileges/src/resolve.rs:201/232. Never constructed from a real catalog.
`rg -i 'all_roles|dba_roles|all_role_privs|dba_role_privs' crates/` => no loader; only the missing-permission report string at lib.rs:546 and a doc comment. `intern_role_name` (plsql-core/src/lib.rs:892) is only c
**Repro/trigger:** Triggering input: a schema where an object privilege is granted to a role, e.g. `GRANT SELECT ON app.t TO app_reader_role;` (app_reader_role being a role, not a user). Oracle records an ALL_TAB_PRIVS row {TABLE_SCHEMA=APP, TABLE_NAME=T, GRANTEE=APP_READER_ROLE, PRIVILEGE=SELECT}. apply_grant_row -> grantee_from_dictionary_value("APP_READER_ROLE") returns Grantee::User(APP_READER_ROLE) (never Role, because no role set is loaded). resolve_grants_for_schema then calls grant_confidence which, for Grantee::User, returns (High, None) and the `if let Grantee::Role` block at resolve.rs:97 is skipped, so NO AuthorizationAmbiguity{RuntimeGrantOrRole} is emitted. Expected (Y): Low confidence + a Runtim
**Suggested fix:** Load a role-name set on the snapshot (ALL_ROLES for ordinary users, or DBA_ROLES under SELECT_CATALOG_ROLE), gated by the existing can_query_roles_and_grants capability (lib.rs:544). In grantee_from_dictionary_value, after the PUBLIC check, look the interned name up in that set: if present, return Grantee::Role(RoleName::from(symbol)) via intern_role_name; else Grantee::User. Crucially, honor R13 for the unknown case: when the role set could NOT be loaded (capability absent), do not silently default to Grantee::User — attach a typed UnknownReason / uncertain grantee so the resolver does not assign spurious High confidence to grants of undetermined grantee class. Add a non-synthetic OracleRow fixture test (GRANTEE matching a loaded role) asserting Grantee::Role and that resolve caps it at Low with AuthorizationAmbiguity(RuntimeGrantOrRole). Reconcile docs/components/privileges.md:27 (list

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
design: IMPL PLAN (investigated 2026-06-02): Grantee::Role is never produced; load_catalog_grants queries all_tab_privs (table_schema,table_name,grantee,privilege,grantable,hierarchy) — no type column — and grantee_from_dictionary_value (lib.rs:4144) returns Public for PUBLIC else Grantee::User unconditionally. CatalogSnapshot has NO roles/users set. FIX: (1) add load_catalog_users (or roles) step querying ALL_USERS (username) — widely accessible, no DBA priv needed — into a new snapshot field e.g. known_users: HashSet<UserName> (intern); call it BEFORE load_catalog_grants. (2) grantee_from_dictionary_value: PUBLIC->Public; interned name in known_users->User; else->Role(RoleName::from(symbol)). (3) Graceful degradation: if ALL_USERS unqueryable (capability/priv), fall back to current User-classification (document as best-effort; do NOT fail the snapshot). (4) Update synthetic test fixtures (lib.rs ~5906/6335/6343 build fake GRANTEE rows incl REPORTING_ROLE) to also provide a users fixture, and UPDATE existing tests at ~6244/6616 which currently assert REPORTING_ROLE is Grantee::User(_) -> expect Grantee::Role(_). (5) Add regression test: a grant to a role name not in all_users classifies as Role; a grant to a real user classifies as User. Consider RoleName interning parity with UserName. Verify privilege-chain consumers (via_role) still behave.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit 49454fd7b8), re-verified on main. Root cause fixed: catalog grant extraction now classifies ALL_TAB_PRIVS grantees against an ALL_USERS-derived known_users set instead of always returning Grantee::User. New load_catalog_users() queries ALL_USERS (PUBLIC-readable, no DBA priv) and is called before load_catalog_grants in load_snapshot_from_connection; failure is non-fatal and records an all-users-probe CapabilityWarning leaving known_users==None. grantee_from_dictionary_value: PUBLIC->Public; known user->User; otherwise->Role (the only remaining grantee class). R13/fail-toward-restrictive: the undetermined case (known_users==None) classifies as Role, NOT the previous fail-toward-permissive high-confidence Grantee::User, so the

title: plsql-ir: propagate intra-procedural taint transitively through locals (use-def fixpoint) + wire analyze_flow
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs:107 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** None today — the taint pipeline (analyze_flow → run_scan → SEC001) has no production caller, so the false negative cannot mislead any shipped scan. If/when SEC001 is wired into a real source scan, two-step laundering through an intermediate local (v_tmp := p_user; v_sql := v_tmp;) would let an EXECUTE IMMEDIATE built from v_sql escape SEC001 (injection false negative).
**Evidence:** crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs:107-114 walk() handles Statement::Assignment by `let flow = expr_flow(&rhs_expr, sources); env.merge_into(target, flow);` — `sources` (TaintSources) is the immutable initial set; the tainted target is merged into `env`, never appended to `sources`, and there is no fixpoint re-walk. collect_expr_flow (lines 146-164) taints an Expr::Name only when its head matches `sources.user_input_names`/`bind_names`; it never consults `env`. So for `v_sql := v_tmp`, v_tmp is resolved against sources={p_user}, no match, empty flow → v_sql untainted.\n\nWiring proof (rg over crates/ tools/, --type rust): harness `run_scan` and `analyze_flow` are referenced ONLY inside #[cfg(test)] modules (rules.rs:1176+, false_positive.rs:92 `mod tests`, rules_qual.rs:176, harness.rs:188 tests, flow_query.rs:124 tests, flow_intra.rs:191 tests). `Sec001ExecuteImmediateInjection` is insta
**Repro/trigger:** Trigger (latent): sources={p_user}; body `v_tmp := p_user; v_sql := v_tmp;`. Processing v_tmp: expr_flow matches p_user in sources → v_tmp tainted UserInput in env. Processing v_sql: expr_flow(v_tmp, sources={p_user}) — v_tmp not in sources, env not consulted → empty flow → v_sql.taint.kinds empty → FlowQuery::taint_of("v_sql").is_tainted == false. SEC001 (rules.rs:83) sees is_tainted=false and emits no injection finding. Confirmed by the project's own existing test pattern (flow_query.rs:130 `env()` helper drives the same path) and unambiguous from the data flow. Disproof of live impact: no non-test code constructs ScanUnit/FlowQuery/analyze_flow or calls harness run_scan, so no shipped sca
**Suggested fix:** Make analyze_flow propagate taint transitively: track an evolving set of tainted/cleansed names as walk() proceeds, and in collect_expr_flow consult env.get(head) (the live FlowEnv) in addition to the static `sources`, unioning an already-tainted local's kinds/cleansers into the current expression's flow. Because the body re-reads names in loops/branches, iterate walk() to a fixpoint until FlowEnv stops changing (lattice is finite → terminates). Add a regression test asserting N-step laundering (v_tmp := p_user; v_sql := v_tmp; EXECUTE IMMEDIATE v_sql) flags SEC001. Note: this is defense-in-depth — also schedule the work to actually wire analyze_flow + run_scan + Sec001 into a production scan entry point, since no shipped path currently invokes them.

### Site crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs:146 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** None today — the analyze_flow -> FlowQuery -> SEC001 -> run_scan pipeline has no production caller (all callers are #[cfg(test)] or dead helpers), so no shipped scan can be fooled. The latent risk: once the SAST pipeline is wired to feed whole-routine flow envs to SEC001, the common "stage user input in a local, then EXECUTE IMMEDIATE the local" pattern would be silently judged clean (taint false negative) on any one-hop variable indirection.
**Evidence:** flow_intra.rs:138 `fn expr_flow(expr: &Expr, sources: &TaintSources) -> ValueFlow` — env is never a parameter. flow_intra.rs:144-164 collect_expr_flow's `Expr::Name(n)` arm matches `head` ONLY against `sources.user_input_names`/`sources.bind_names`; it never calls `env.get(head)`. walk() (107-134) only WRITES env via `env.merge_into(target, flow)` (line 113) and passes env nowhere into expr_flow.

Empirical (temporary test injected then reverted): `cargo test -p plsql-ir TEMP_two_hop_local_chain -- --nocapture` on body "v_a := p_user; v_sql := v_a;" with p_user as a UserInput source printed:
  V_A tainted? [UserInput]
  V_SQL tainted? []
=> the second local is NOT tainted. test FAILED at the v_sql assertion. Edit reverted; `rg -c 'TEMP_two_hop' crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs` => 0.

Wiring: `rg 'analyze_flow'` shows every caller besides the def/re-export is inside a test module (flow_
**Repro/trigger:** Triggering input (proves the code defect): stmts = lower_statement_body("v_a := p_user; v_sql := v_a;"), sources.user_input_names = ["p_user"]. Expected (sound may-analysis): env.get("v_sql").taint.kinds contains UserInput. Actual: env.get("v_a") = [UserInput] but env.get("v_sql") = [] (verified by running the temp test above). Disproof of LIVE security impact: no production code path reaches analyze_flow or SEC001 — the entire flow->SEC001->run_scan chain is exercised only by unit tests, and the only production crate consuming plsql-sast (plsql-mcp) calls just to_sarif on a ScanReport. Therefore X->wrong-Y exists at the unit level but cannot be triggered by any shipped user action today.
**Suggested fix:** Thread the accumulated &FlowEnv (read before merging the current LHS) into expr_flow/collect_expr_flow; in the Expr::Name arm, after the sources checks, also do `if let Some(prev) = env.get(head) { union prev.taint.kinds, prev.taint.cleansed_by, and join prev.value_set/string_shape into flow }`. A single forward pass over the flat list suffices for straight-line bodies; the existing recursive walk + ValueSet::join keeps branches/loops a sound may-analysis. Add regression tests: two-hop (v_a:=p_user; v_sql:=v_a => v_sql tainted) and cleansed-then-reused. Separately, when the real SAST pipeline is wired, ensure DynamicSqlEvidence.site is the upper-cased FlowEnv variable key (today fact_emit doc says it is a unit-logical-id+fragment summary, which would not key into FlowEnv at all) — otherwise even the transitive fix will not connect site -> taint. This is a pre-wiring correctness gap, not 

### Site crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs:101 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** The shipped guard product never runs intra-procedural taint analysis on real source: the production engine path always sets an empty FlowSummary, so injection-detection via the FlowEnv/run_scan pipeline is an absent capability rather than a wrong result. No user gets an incorrect or unsafe scan output today; they just get no taint findings, and the gap is self-documented in-code as deferred to a later bead.
**Evidence:** analyze_flow is a complete, compiling implementation (NOT a stub): `cargo build -p plsql-ir` => "Finished dev profile" with no errors; flow_intra.rs:101-130 walks Assignment/If/ForLoop/WhileLoop/BareLoop, lowers RHS, merges taint via ValueSet::join. The divergent-path substrate is confirmed: every analyze_flow call site is inside #[cfg(test)] — flow_intra.rs:206-316 (own tests), flow_query.rs:127 (mod tests opens 124), plsql-sast false_positive.rs:108/126 (mod tests opens 92), rules.rs:1314/1379 (mod tests opens 1173). run_scan (harness.rs:105) has zero non-test callers; every ScanUnit is built in tests (rules.rs, rules_qual.rs mod tests opens 173, false_positive.rs). measure_false_positives (false_positive.rs:66, pub) is only called from its own #[cfg(test)] tests (197/253/262/280). The single MCP touchpoint run_sarif_scan (change_tools.rs:41) only calls plsql_sast::to_sarif on a caller
**Repro/trigger:** Disproof that it is a stub: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$PWD/target cargo build -p plsql-ir` finishes clean; flow_intra.rs:101 has a real body (no todo!/unimplemented!/placeholder) and 11 passing unit tests. Demonstration of the wiring gap: `rg -n 'analyze_flow' crates/ --type rust` shows every non-definition reference is inside a #[cfg(test)] mod; `rg -n 'taint_path_count' crates/` shows only the struct-field declaration at lib.rs:101 and no assignment; the production lowering at lib.rs:1087 unconditionally assigns FlowSummary::default(). Therefore no input can make a shipped binary emit a non-empty taint/flow summary — the capability is unreachable in production, which is a completeness gap, not a c
**Suggested fix:** Leave flow_intra.rs:101 as-is — it is correct and complete. The actionable item is a wiring bead in plsql-engine: at lib.rs:1087, replace the unconditional `run.flow_summary = FlowSummary::default();` by deriving TaintSources per lowered routine (public IN params -> user_input_names, binds -> bind_names), calling plsql_ir::analyze_flow to build a FlowEnv, projecting via FlowQuery to aggregate real taint_path_count/string_shape_count, and exposing the FlowEnvs so the MCP sarif_scan path can construct ScanUnit{flow} and invoke plsql_sast::run_scan to produce a populated ScanReport. If still deferred, make the deferral explicit at the API boundary (document that flow_summary is not yet computed and that sarif_scan requires an externally produced ScanReport) so callers do not assume injection detection runs. Also fix the misclassification: this is not a "genuine-stub".

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit 51b99a3eed), re-verified on main. Implemented the use-def transitive fix in production code: threaded the accumulated &FlowEnv into expr_flow/collect_expr_flow; in the Expr::Name arm, after the static `sources` checks, consult env.get(head) and union the prior local's LIVE taint kinds + cleansed_by (and carry string_shape if the parent has none). Made analyze_flow iterate walk() to a fixpoint over the finite taint lattice (capped at 64 passes as a belt-and-suspenders bound) so a name tainted only on a later pass (loop back-edge / forward ref) is captured. This closes the v_tmp := p_user; v_sql := v_tmp; laundering false negative. The change only strengthens taint propagation (more sound, never less), so no existing test or t

title: plsql-symbols dynamic_sql_confidence: tighten Medium tier (require DBMS_ASSERT coverage of all interpolations)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-symbols/src/dynamic_sql_confidence.rs:75 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** No live user-facing impact today: the over-trusting Medium tier is computed by score_dynamic_sql_edge/enrich_dynamic_sql but has zero production consumers (only a lib.rs re-export and unit tests), and the authoritative SEC001 injection verdict uses independent taint analysis. The defect is a latent precision/honesty flaw: the moment the dynamic-SQL confidence tier is wired into graph/lineage/report output, a partially-sanitised EXECUTE IMMEDIATE will be stamped Medium and labelled "object set is bounded by the asserted names" while an unsanitised interpolation still permits injection.
**Evidence:** dynamic_sql_confidence.rs:75-92 — `fn medium_or_low(evidence)`: `if !evidence.dbms_assert_calls.is_empty() { Confidence { level: ConfidenceLevel::Medium, explanation: Some(format!("interpolated dynamic SQL, {} identifier(s) routed through DBMS_ASSERT — object set is bounded by the asserted names", evidence.dbms_assert_calls.len())) } } else { ... Low ... }`. Medium is awarded on a non-empty count with NO coverage comparison against the number of `<expr>` fragments. This contradicts the module doc (lines 16-19): "ContainsExpression but EVERY interpolated identifier is wrapped in a DBMS_ASSERT sanitiser → Medium".

dynamic_sql.rs:44 — `pub dbms_assert_calls: Vec<DbmsAssertCall>` is a flat list; `detect_dbms_assert_calls` (271-304) is a purely textual scan with no linkage to which `<expr>` it sanitises.

Empirical repro (throwaway integration test, since removed) output:
  dbms_assert_calls
**Repro/trigger:** Triggering input (verified): `EXECUTE IMMEDIATE 'SELECT * FROM ' || DBMS_ASSERT.SIMPLE_SQL_NAME(p_tab) || ' WHERE c=''' || p_raw || ''''` → recognise_dynamic_sql yields dbms_assert_calls.len()==1, two `<expr>` fragments, and score_dynamic_sql_edge returns ConfidenceLevel::Medium with "object set is bounded by the asserted names" even though p_raw is unsanitised. Disproof of the high-impact framing: a full reference trace shows the only path out of medium_or_low (score_dynamic_sql_edge → enrich_dynamic_sql → EnrichedDynamicSql.refined_confidence) terminates at a public re-export with no in-tree production consumer; SEC001 (the must-fix verdict) reads taint_of(), not this score; so no live rep
**Suggested fix:** In medium_or_low, do not award Medium on a bare non-empty dbms_assert_calls list. As an interim heuristic, count `<expr>` fragments and only return Medium when assert_count >= expr_count (else Low with a "partial sanitisation (N of M)" explanation). The robust fix is to give the recogniser per-interpolation linkage: when extract_execute_immediate collapses a non-literal run into an `<expr>` placeholder, record whether that specific run was a DBMS_ASSERT call, and have medium_or_low award Medium only when every `<expr>` is so marked. Lock in with a regression test using the `... SIMPLE_SQL_NAME(p_tab) ... || p_raw || ...` example asserting ConfidenceLevel::Low, and correct the explanation string so it no longer claims "object set is bounded" under partial sanitisation. Priority is low/defense-in-depth: fix before wiring the confidence tier into any graph/lineage/MCP report.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit c198412c7b), re-verified on main. Root-cause fix using the robust per-interpolation linkage from the suggested fix (not just the interim count heuristic). The recogniser now captures each non-literal concatenation run and tags it via a new ExprInterpolation{sanitised} entry (Vec<ExprInterpolation> on DynamicSqlEvidence) according to whether that specific run contains a DBMS_ASSERT.<fn>(...) call. medium_or_low now awards Medium only when total>0 && sanitised==total; partial coverage stays Low with a 'partial DBMS_ASSERT sanitisation (N of M)' explanation that no longer claims 'object set is bounded'; the full-coverage Medium explanation says 'all N interpolation(s) routed through DBMS_ASSERT'. Verified the regression test fa

title: plsql-parser-antlr recover.rs: add leading word-boundary check to matches_kw_at (parity)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-parser-antlr/src/recover.rs:191 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** None today: recover_to_statement_boundary has no production caller (test-only), so no shipping behavior is affected. If wired into the parse pipeline, error recovery would over/under-shoot the statement boundary for sources where an identifier ends in END/BEGIN inside a live BEGIN..END block (e.g. `BEGIN x_END; END;`), landing the diagnostic span one statement early — a degraded best-effort diagnostic, never a crash or data corruption.
**Evidence:** recover.rs:191-202 matches_kw_at checks ONLY the trailing boundary:
  198: if end < bytes.len() && bytes[end].is_ascii_alphanumeric() { return false; }
  (no check on bytes[pos-1]; trailing class also omits _ $ #)
Sibling lower/mod.rs:1093-1114 matches_keyword_ignore_case checks BOTH:
  1104: let is_ident_cont = |c: u8| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == b'_' || c == b'$' || c == b'#';
  1105: if end < bytes.len() && is_ident_cont(bytes[end]) { return false; }
  1110: if pos > 0 && is_ident_cont(bytes[pos - 1]) { return false; }
Caller search (rg over crates/): recover_to_statement_boundary appears only in recover.rs; every call site is at line 233+ which is below the `#[cfg(test)]` marker at line 231. Declared `pub mod recover` in lib.rs:76 but no non-test caller anywhere in the workspace.
Build: `cargo build -p plsql-parser-antlr` -> Finished dev profile (compiles clean; not a compile-l
**Repro/trigger:** Standalone Rust port of the scanner core (rustc -O /tmp/repro_recover.rs):
input: "bad BEGIN x_END; END; rest"
  buggy  recovered_at = 16  -> "bad BEGIN x_END;"   (END suffix of x_END falsely matched, depth 1->0, first ';' terminates early)
  fixed  recovered_at = 21  -> "bad BEGIN x_END; END;"  (with leading-boundary check, depth stays 1 until real END)
Caveat verified: non-nested `bad MY_END; rest` recovers at 11 unchanged because the spurious END decrement is clamped by `if depth > 0` (recover.rs:144) — only nested cases with a live BEGIN exhibit the defect.
**Suggested fix:** Mirror lower/mod.rs:1093-1114: introduce `let is_ident_cont = |c: u8| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == b'_' || c == b'$' || c == b'#';`, apply it to the trailing check (replacing the bare is_ascii_alphanumeric) AND add the leading check `if pos > 0 && is_ident_cont(bytes[pos - 1]) { return false; }`. Add a regression test asserting `bad BEGIN x_END; END; rest` recovers after the SECOND `;`. Low priority / defense-in-depth: harmless until recover_to_statement_boundary gains a production caller.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit 548fe539bb), re-verified on main. Defense-in-depth bead (recover_to_statement_boundary is test-only today, no production caller), but the fix has no baseline conflict so I implemented the full root-cause fix rather than deferring. recover::matches_kw_at checked only the trailing boundary and used a too-narrow trailing class (bare is_ascii_alphanumeric). Fix mirrors lower::matches_keyword_ignore_case: is_ident_cont = alnum | _ | $ | #, applied to the trailing check AND a new leading-boundary check (pos>0 && is_ident_cont(bytes[pos-1])). Now x_END's END suffix and BEGIN_OF_DAY's BEGIN prefix no longer perturb BEGIN/END depth. Verified both regression tests FAIL before (recovered_at 16 vs 21; 25 vs 20) and PASS after, by tempor

title: Wire plsql-sast run_scan into engine/MCP sarif_scan pipeline (SAST engine currently unreachable)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-sast/src/harness.rs:105 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** The SAST rule engine (run_scan) is real and fully unit-tested but not yet wired into the engine/MCP pipeline, so the advertised `sarif_scan` MCP tool can never return real findings today — a roadmap/integration gap, not incorrect runtime behavior. No user gets a wrong or unsafe answer: sarif_scan is honestly gated as RuntimeStateRequired rather than emitting empty/fabricated findings.
**Evidence:** grep across workspace for plsql_sast::run_scan callers: every non-definition reference is test/false-positive code:
  crates/plsql-sast/src/false_positive.rs:80 (measure_false_positives, itself only called from #[cfg(test)] at false_positive.rs:197/253/262/280)
  crates/plsql-sast/src/rules.rs:1176+ (inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`)
  crates/plsql-sast/src/rules_qual.rs:176+ (test mod)
  crates/plsql-sast/src/harness.rs:278-390 (test mod)
  crates/plsql-sast/tests/zz_probe_sec001_failopen.rs:38 (integration test)
tools/usr-loop/src/main.rs:575 `fn run_scan` is an UNRELATED function — `rg 'plsql-sast' tools/usr-loop/Cargo.toml` => exit 1 (no dep).
Engine does not depend on SAST: `rg 'plsql-sast|plsql_sast' crates/plsql-engine/` => exit 1. `rg 'plsql-sast' crates/*/Cargo.toml` => only crates/plsql-mcp/Cargo.toml:41.
MCP sarif_scan dispatch is validate-then-gate (crates/plsql-mcp/src/dispat
**Repro/trigger:** Impossibility-of-correctness-bug proof: run_scan (harness.rs:105) cannot misbehave because it has no production input path — there is no X that reaches it outside tests. The line is correct as written wherever exercised (121 unit tests pass per claim). The product-level effect (sarif_scan returns no findings) is not a wrong answer: dispatch.rs:260 returns RuntimeStateRequired, a truthful "needs analysis state" gate, not a fail-open empty result. Thus no X -> wrong-output Y exists; this is an unreachable-but-correct, honestly-gated feature = integration/roadmap gap, classified defense-in-depth per protocol step 3 (intentional public API seam for a later phase).
**Suggested fix:** Treat as a deferred integration bead, not a correctness fix. Either (a) add plsql-sast as a plsql-engine dep, map AnalysisRun->CompletenessSnapshot, build &[ScanUnit] from per-unit FlowEnvs, assemble the rule registry, call run_scan, and thread the resulting ScanReport into the MCP sarif_scan handler (replacing the RuntimeStateRequired gate at dispatch.rs:256-263) plus an end-to-end fixture test; OR (b) if SAST integration is genuinely post-phase, mark `sast`/`sarif_scan` as "planned" in README.md:261 and the MCP listing docs so advertised capability matches reality. No change to harness.rs:105 itself is warranted — the code is correct.

title: plsql-accretion G8: tighten residue is_synthetic_alias to fixture.rs strict shape
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-accretion/src/gate.rs:621 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** G8 (the independent privacy backstop that re-scans every MinFixture in fixtures_dir) would PASS a fixture containing an un-scrubbed numeric secret (16-digit card, decimal salary, SSN-shape) or a spoofed/short id_/sx_ alias, instead of aborting the run — but only for a fixture that bypassed the strict build-time check (builder regression, future scrub change, or a hand-placed/tampered fixture), since the normal pipeline strict-checks fixtures before they reach the corpus.
**Evidence:** gate.rs:621-630 (loose):
  fn is_synthetic_alias(body)->bool { let b=trim..; if let Some(rest)=b.strip_prefix("id_").or(sx_) { return !rest.is_empty() && rest.bytes().all(is_ascii_alphanumeric); } !b.is_empty() && b.bytes().all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit() || c==b'.' || c==b'-') }
fixture.rs:554-562 (strict) + caller fixture.rs:529 `is_synthetic_alias(body) || body=="7" || body=="7.0"`:
  id_ -> hex.len()==12 && all hexdigit; sx_ -> hex.len()==8 && all hexdigit; else false.
tokscrub.rs:168-174 synthesise(Class::Num,_) emits ONLY "7"/"7.0". tokscrub.rs:101 NumericLiteral->Class::Num. tokscrub.rs:362 EstateClass(t.text.clone()) carries verbatim text to G8.
gate.rs:619 comment falsely claims it "Mirrors fixture.rs's synthesis vocabulary"; gate.rs:1149-1151 test asserts is_synthetic_alias("123") and ("sx_42a") TRUE.
Standalone compiled side-by-side (rustc -O /tmp/divergence_check.rs):
  input     
**Repro/trigger:** Trigger: place a fixture file in fixtures_dir whose source contains a bare numeric secret, e.g. `SELECT 4111111111111111 FROM dual;` (or `'spoofed'` aliased as id_abc), then call gate::residue_check(candidate, fixtures_dir). The NumericLiteral classifies as Class::Num -> TokVerdict::EstateClass("4111111111111111") -> gate.rs is_synthetic_alias returns true (all-digit clause) -> NO leak reported, residue_check returns Ok. The same body run through fixture.rs::privacy_residue_clean returns false (leak). Proven by the compiled divergence table above. Not exploitable via the normal builder path because build_min_fixture's strict privacy_residue_clean (fixture.rs:373) rejects such a fixture befor
**Suggested fix:** Replace gate.rs:621-630 with the strict shape used in fixture.rs: id_ -> exactly 12 ascii-hexdigits; sx_ -> exactly 8 ascii-hexdigits; numerals accept ONLY "7" and "7.0" (the two synthesise outputs); everything else fails closed. Extract a single shared is_synthetic_alias into tokscrub.rs (next to synthesise) and have both fixture.rs and gate.rs import it so the two privacy layers cannot drift again. Correct/remove the false "Mirrors fixture.rs's synthesis vocabulary" comment at gate.rs:619. Update the gate.rs unit test synthetic_alias_classifier (1149-1153) to assert the strict contract (id_0123456789ab / sx_0123abcd TRUE; 123, sx_42a, id_abc, id_zzzzzzzzzzzz, 4111111111111111, 98765.43, 555-12-3456 FALSE), and add a G8 self-test that plants a fixture with a bare 16-digit numeral and asserts a PrivacyAbort.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit 4174e81da5), re-verified on main. Extracted a single shared strict `pub fn is_synthetic_alias` into tokscrub.rs (next to synthesise): id_<hex12> / sx_<hex8> / exactly `7`|`7.0`, delimiters trimmed, everything else fails closed. Removed both local duplicates (fixture.rs's strict one and gate.rs's LOOSE one) and pointed both privacy layers at the shared fn so they can't drift. The gate.rs loose version had an all-digit clause that classified a 16-digit card / SSN / decimal salary as a 'synthetic numeral' and a `id_/sx_ + any-alphanumeric-of-any-length` clause accepting `sx_42a`/`id_abc` — both now rejected. Corrected the false 'Mirrors fixture.rs's synthesis vocabulary' comment (deleted with the loose fn). VERIFIED the new G8

title: plsql-ir FlowEnv::merge_into: intersect (not union) taint cleansers across branch joins
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs:68 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** None for any current user: the IR-level over-cleansing is real, but the SAST pipeline that would turn it into a SEC001 false negative (analyze_flow -> DynamicSqlEvidence facts -> run_scan -> SEC001) has no production caller — it is exercised only by tests. The gap becomes a real fail-open only once the scan engine is wired into a production entry point, so it is a latent hardening item, not a live injection bypass.
**Evidence:** IR mechanism reproduced via throwaway test crates/plsql-ir/tests/zz_throwaway_taint_repro.rs (since removed): for "IF c THEN v := DBMS_ASSERT.SIMPLE_SQL_NAME(p_user); ELSE v := p_user; END IF;" with p_user tainted, output was: `kinds=[UserInput] cleansed_by=[DbmsAssert] flags_alarm=false`. Code confirms it: flow_intra.rs:59-72 merge_into unions kinds (63-67) AND cleansers (68-72) into one entry; walk (115-125) recurses both IF arms + else into the same env. flow.rs:164-166 `flags_alarm = !kinds.is_empty() && cleansed_by.is_empty()`; flow_query.rs:80 same for taint_of; rules.rs:83-84 SEC001 gates on `answer.is_tainted`.

REACHABILITY (decisive): `grep -rn analyze_flow crates/` → every hit is a #[cfg(test)] test (flow_intra.rs:206..316, flow_query.rs:132, false_positive.rs:108/126, rules.rs:1314/1379); NO production caller. `grep -rn run_scan crates/` → all callers in harness.rs/rules_qual
**Repro/trigger:** Trigger (IR level, real): analyze_flow over `IF c THEN v := DBMS_ASSERT.SIMPLE_SQL_NAME(p_user); ELSE v := p_user; END IF;` with user_input_names=["p_user"] → FlowEnv["V"].taint = {kinds:[UserInput], cleansed_by:[DbmsAssert]}, flags_alarm()==false despite the uncleansed ELSE path. Disproof of end-to-end Critical impact: there is no production code path from parsed PL/SQL to SEC001 — analyze_flow, run_scan, and emit_dynamic_sql_facts are all only invoked from test modules, and the MCP server only converts a pre-built ScanReport to SARIF (default/empty in production). Therefore no real user input X produces a wrong scan verdict Y in the shipped product today.
**Suggested fix:** Before the SAST scan path (analyze_flow -> DynamicSqlEvidence -> run_scan -> SEC001) is wired into any production entry point, harden the cleanser merge so it cannot fail open: make branch-join cleanser propagation path-AND. As the finding notes, a correct fix needs CFG join points (sequential overwrite must kill a cleanser, branch join must intersect); the simplest CFG-free interim is to DROP cleansers at IF/loop branch joins (union kinds, treat join as 'cleansing not guaranteed'), accepting more false positives to close the fail-open. Until then, correct the flow_intra.rs:6-10 and flags_alarm docs to state cleanser-union is unsound (fail-open) for conditionally-cleansed values, and add a regression test mirroring the repro asserting flags_alarm()/SEC001 fires. Priority is low because the consuming engine is currently test-only; raise to medium the moment run_scan/analyze_flow gain a pr

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [already-resolved] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit 51b99a3eed), re-verified on main. Verified already resolved by bead .1's live-kinds Taint model. The original fear: FlowEnv::merge_into UNIONS cleansers across branch arms, so a sibling cleanse could mask a live kind. But under the current model Taint.kinds holds only LIVE (uncleansed) taint and flags_alarm() = !kinds.is_empty() (no longer gated on cleansed_by). Traced the exact repro `IF c THEN v := DBMS_ASSERT.SIMPLE_SQL_NAME(p_user); ELSE v := p_user; END IF;`: the THEN arm yields {kinds:[], cleansed_by:[DbmsAssert]}, the ELSE arm yields {kinds:[UserInput]}; merge_into produces {kinds:[UserInput], cleansed_by:[DbmsAssert]} → flags_alarm()=true (the live ELSE-path kind survives). Under the OLD `!kinds.is_empty() && cleanse

title: plsql-bindgen disambiguate_overloads: handle non-empty suffix collisions
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-bindgen/src/overload.rs:74 — DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (latent/phase-gated), severity=low
**Impact:** None today — the function has no production caller, so no non-compiling Rust is generated; the live emitter uses r.name directly and routes real overload collisions to the OverloadAmbiguity wrapper-skip diagnostic. It would only break (emit two identical `pub fn` names) if this exported public API were later wired into the generator for type-only overloads sharing a parameter name alongside a differently-named sibling.
**Evidence:** overload.rs:71-83 — the per-overload guard is `if suffix.is_empty() { ...ordinal... } else { out[*idx] = format!("{base_name}_{suffix}") }`; it never detects two equal non-empty suffixes. Lines 76-78 state the broken contract: "fall back to an ordinal suffix so the emitter never produces two identical Rust names." Standalone executable mirroring the exact algorithm on input proc(p_val),proc(p_val),proc(p_other): `names = ["proc_p_val", "proc_p_val", "proc_p_other"]; unique count = 2 (expected 3); COLLISION = true`. Caller audit: `rg disambiguate_overloads` → only its own definition + unit tests in overload.rs and a single `pub use overload::disambiguate_overloads;` at lib.rs:34; no other call site. Emitter writes names directly: emit.rs:148 `write!(out, "pub fn {}(executor: &mut impl OracleExecutor", r.name)`. Real collisions handled separately by `OverloadAmbiguity` ("wrapper skipped") 
**Repro/trigger:** Triggering input: disambiguate_overloads(&[rb("proc", vec!["p_val"]), rb("proc", vec!["p_val"]), rb("proc", vec!["p_other"])]). shared_prefix_length=0 because groups[2][0]="p_other" != "p_val" breaks the prefix loop at index 0; suffixes = ["p_val","p_val","p_other"], none empty, so out = ["proc_p_val","proc_p_val","proc_p_other"] — duplicate. Confirmed via compiled standalone (unique count 2 of 3). Impossibility of current harm: grep proves no non-test caller exists; emit_wrappers (emit.rs:148) never invokes the function and the generator routes overload collisions to the OverloadAmbiguity skip path, so no non-compiling Rust reaches output today.
**Suggested fix:** In disambiguate_overloads, build candidate names first (empty-suffix → ordinal as today, else base_p_suffix), then run a second pass that detects any name occurring more than once within the group and appends "_<i+1>" to each colliding occurrence (deterministic for fixed input order, preserving the documented stability property). Add a regression test mirroring the repro asserting all three names are unique. Lower priority since the function is currently unwired; fix is warranted because it is exported public API that breaks its own line-77 uniqueness contract and would emit non-compiling Rust if wired in.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit 50b466b216), re-verified on main. ROOT-CAUSE fix (defense-in-depth; function remains unwired in the live emitter, which uses r.name directly and routes real overload collisions to the OverloadAmbiguity skip path, so zero behavior change today). disambiguate_overloads now builds candidate names in pass 1 (empty suffix -> ordinal, else base_suffix) then runs a pass 2 that detects any candidate occurring more than once within the group and appends a deterministic _<i+1> (input-order index within the group) to each colliding occurrence. This upholds the line-77 'never two identical Rust names' contract even when the shared-prefix scan stops at index 0 (equal non-empty suffixes), preserves the documented input-order stability pro

title: oraclemcp-guard classifier.analyze_batch: make keyword/depth tally quote_style-aware
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs:209 — CONFIRMED DEFECT, severity=high
**Impact:** An injected multi-statement batch can hide a real statement-boundary desync (e.g. a stray top-level END or an unterminated PL/SQL block) behind a balanced double-quoted identifier decoy like "BEGIN"/"END", causing the guard to downgrade a batch the fail-closed law mandates as Forbidden down to a dispatchable Guarded tier; secondarily, valid read-only queries selecting a quoted column named "END"/"BEGIN" are wrongly rejected as Forbidden.
**Evidence:** crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs:207-234 — the analyze_batch loop matches structural keywords purely on the uppercased word value and never inspects quote_style:
  Token::Word(w) => { match w.value.to_ascii_uppercase().as_str() {
      "BEGIN" => { depth += 1; ... } "DECLARE" => {...}
      "IF" | "CASE" | "LOOP" => { if !expecting_close { depth += 1; } ... }
      "END" => { depth -= 1; if depth < 0 { went_negative = true; } ... }
      _ => expecting_close = false, } segment_has_content = true; }
balanced is computed as `depth == 0 && !went_negative` (line 258), and classify() gates Forbidden on `!shape.balanced` at lines 551-554 and 572-576.

Empirical run (throwaway integration test, cargo test -p oraclemcp-guard -- --nocapture):
  PROBE quoted BEGIN: value="BEGIN" quote_style=Some('"') keyword=NoKeyword
  BASELINE 'SELECT 1 FROM dual; END;' => Forbidden
  MASK shape balanced
**Repro/trigger:** Triggering input X -> wrong output Y, proven:
1) X = `SELECT "BEGIN" FROM dual; END;` -> Y = Guarded (balanced=true). The quoted "BEGIN" adds +1 depth, cancelling the genuine stray top-level END (-1); the desync that should be Forbidden is masked. Baseline `SELECT 1 FROM dual; END;` correctly returns Forbidden, isolating the cause to the quoted token.
2) X = `BEGIN UPDATE t SET x=1 WHERE id=2; "END"` -> Y = Guarded (balanced=true); an unterminated real PL/SQL block reads as balanced because only the quoted "END" identifier closes the real BEGIN.
3) X = `SELECT "END" FROM dual` -> Y = Forbidden, even though sqlparser parses it cleanly as one read-only Query (over-block false positive).
Tokeni
**Suggested fix:** In analyze_batch's `Token::Word(w)` arm, short-circuit when `w.quote_style.is_some()`: treat a quoted word as an ordinary identifier (set expecting_close=false, segment_has_content=true) and skip the BEGIN/DECLARE/IF/CASE/LOOP/END depth tally — exactly as string literals are already excluded by being single tokens. This makes `SELECT "BEGIN" FROM dual; END;` report balanced=false (the real stray END desyncs) -> Forbidden, restores the fail-closed law for `BEGIN ...; "END"`, and lets `SELECT "END" FROM dual` classify as a single read-only Query. Add regression tests for both directions (masked stray END must be Forbidden; SELECT "END"/"BEGIN" must be a Query). user_defined_calls already filters on keyword != NoKeyword and is not affected by this masking direction; a quote_style note there is optional hygiene.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: analyze_batch now ignores keyword interpretation for delimited (quoted) identifiers via w.quote_style: a quoted "BEGIN"/"END" is a column name, not a PL/SQL keyword, so it no longer moves the block-depth counter. Closes the fail-closed-desync defeat where SELECT "BEGIN" FROM dual; END; rebalanced to Guarded instead of Forbidden. Added regression test quoted_keyword_identifier_does_not_move_block_depth; 59 guard lib tests green, clippy -D warnings clean, boundary lint OK.

title: plsql-mcp live-xe test: add missing `deadline` field to EnableWritesToken (E0063 unblocks founder Oracle gate)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-mcp/tests/mcp_live_xe.rs:775 — CONFIRMED DEFECT, severity=high
**Impact:** The entire `live-xe` integration test target fails to compile, so none of the real-Oracle E2E tests in mcp_live_xe.rs can run — the founder's opt-in verification path against the Oracle XE 23ai container is fully blocked until one line is added.
**Evidence:** Production struct (crates/plsql-mcp/src/safety.rs:104-128): `#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]` (no Default) on `pub struct EnableWritesToken { token, connection, operation_summary, issued_at, ttl_seconds, #[serde(skip)] pub deadline: Option<MonotonicDeadline> }`. The `#[serde(skip)]` affects only (de)serialization, not struct-literal initialization.

Test literal (crates/plsql-mcp/tests/mcp_live_xe.rs:775-784) supplies only 5 fields (token, connection, operation_summary, issued_at, ttl_seconds) and omits `deadline`. `rg deadline` over the test file returns zero matches.

Compile (ran): `cargo test -p plsql-mcp --features live-xe --test mcp_live_xe --no-run` →
  error[E0063]: missing field `deadline` in initializer of `EnableWritesToken`
  error: could not compile `plsql-mcp` (test "mcp_live_xe") due to 1 previous error

Default-feature compile (ran): `cargo 
**Repro/trigger:** Triggering input: `cargo test -p plsql-mcp --features live-xe --test mcp_live_xe --no-run` → E0063 missing field `deadline` at mcp_live_xe.rs:775:35, "could not compile". Disproof of "slips past gates" half: the same command without `--features live-xe` exits 0. Fix is the suggested_fix one-liner; after it the suite should compile.
**Suggested fix:** After the `ttl_seconds: ENABLE_WRITES_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS,` line (783) insert a live deadline so the defense-in-depth assertion is not defeated by incidental expiry: `deadline: Some(<re-exported MonotonicDeadline>::after(std::time::Duration::from_secs(ENABLE_WRITES_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS))),`. Resolve the actual path the test sees for MonotonicDeadline (production imports it from oraclemcp_guard; confirm whether plsql_mcp re-exports it before settling on plsql_mcp::MonotonicDeadline vs oraclemcp_guard::MonotonicDeadline). Then re-run `cargo test -p plsql-mcp --features live-xe --test mcp_live_xe --no-run` to confirm clean. Follow-up: add a `--features live-xe --no-run` lane to CI so feature-gated compile breaks are caught at the normal gate.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: Fixed E0063: added the missing live MonotonicDeadline to the EnableWritesToken literal in mcp_live_xe.rs:775 (mirrors production mint path), and re-exported plsql_mcp::MonotonicDeadline so the public EnableWritesToken::deadline field type is nameable by consumers/tests. live-xe suite now compiles; refusal_permanently_read_only_blocks_even_with_token passes (refusal is purely PermanentlyReadOnly, not expiry). Default-feature tests green, clippy --all-targets --features live-xe clean. Did NOT run cargo fmt on plsql-mcp per AGENTS.md (rustfmt drift).

title: plsql-mcp create_or_replace: whitespace-tokenize classify_kind/parse_target_schema (cross-schema write bypass)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-mcp/src/create_or_replace.rs:229 — CONFIRMED DEFECT, severity=high
**Impact:** An agent connected as BILLING can submit `CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE␣␣BODY ANALYTICS.INVOICE_PKG ...` (double space / tab / newline between PACKAGE and BODY) with target_schema="" and operator_typed_schema=None, and run_execute_approved approves a cross-schema write into ANALYTICS as confirmed=true SameSchema(BILLING) — the operator-typed-schema confirmation control is silently skipped, so a package body is deployed into a foreign schema without the destination name ever being typed.
**Evidence:** crates/plsql-mcp/src/create_or_replace.rs:183-191 classify_kind loops SUPPORTED_KINDS and matches via `rest.strip_prefix(kind)` where kind=="PACKAGE BODY" is a single-ASCII-space literal; `PACKAGE  BODY` fails that prefix and falls through to the single-word "PACKAGE" branch (after.starts_with(whitespace) passes). create_or_replace.rs:229 `rest.strip_prefix(kind.as_str())` strips only "PACKAGE", so the name token becomes "BODY" (no dot) → returns Ok(None). execute_approved.rs:130-133 maps None to principal_schema; :138-139 skips the agreement check when caller_target.is_empty(); :146-150 calls require_cross_schema_confirmation(BILLING,BILLING,None) which (cross_schema.rs:98-105) returns SameSchema/confirmed. Reproduced end-to-end with a tests/ integration harness using the crate public API (cargo test -p plsql-mcp passed all 3): double-space classify = Ok("PACKAGE"); parse = Ok(None); ru
**Repro/trigger:** Trigger: malicious_ddl = "CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE  BODY ANALYTICS.INVOICE_PKG AS\nBEGIN\n  NULL;\nEND INVOICE_PKG;\n" (two spaces). 1) run_create_or_replace DryRun mints token "tok-evil" (classify accepts it as PACKAGE, no error). 2) run_execute_approved{connection:"billing-dev", token:"tok-evil", ddl_bytes:malicious_ddl, principal_schema:"BILLING", target_schema:"", operator_typed_schema:None} → Ok(plan) with plan.cross_schema.confirmed==true, decision SameSchema(BILLING), plan.ddl_bytes still naming ANALYTICS. Oracle's lexer collapses the whitespace run so it compiles a PACKAGE BODY into ANALYTICS. Control (single space) is correctly refused with ConfirmationMissing{target:"ANALYTICS"}. 
**Suggested fix:** Make classify_kind and parse_target_schema whitespace-tolerant: split the post-"CREATE OR REPLACE" remainder into head tokens via split_whitespace(), compare the joined upper-cased first 1-2 tokens against SUPPORTED_KINDS preferring the two-token form ("PACKAGE BODY"/"TYPE BODY") before the one-token form, and advance past the matched token count (not via single-space strip_prefix). Equivalently fail-closed: reject any CREATE OR REPLACE header whose kind keywords are separated by anything other than a single ASCII space (UnsupportedKind), forcing canonical DDL. Add regression tests for `PACKAGE  BODY` (two spaces), `PACKAGE\tBODY`, `PACKAGE\nBODY` with a schema-qualified name asserting classify_kind=="PACKAGE BODY" and parse_target_schema==Some("ANALYTICS"), plus an execute_approved test asserting ConfirmationMissing when no operator schema is typed.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: Rewrote classify_kind + parse_target_schema to tokenize the DDL header on whitespace RUNS (split_whitespace) instead of literal single-space strip_prefix. PACKAGE BODY / TYPE BODY now survive tabs/multiple-spaces/newlines between the two words, so the kind suffix and the schema-qualified owner are no longer dropped — closing the cross-schema write-confirmation bypass (CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE<TAB>BODY OWNER.PKG previously classified as PACKAGE with target_schema=None). Two-word kinds tried before one-word, robust to SUPPORTED_KINDS order. Regression test two_word_kind_survives_arbitrary_whitespace added (5 separators + qualified-name spacing). 14 create_or_replace tests + full plsql-mcp suite green; clippy -D warnings clean. No cargo fmt (plsql-mcp pre-existing).

title: plsql-ir split_statements/strip_comments: make string-literal aware (; inside literal mis-splits)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-ir/src/stmt.rs:196 — CONFIRMED DEFECT, severity=high
**Impact:** On the source-slice fallback path (used when ANTLR yields no body statements), a routine body containing a string literal with embedded `;` or block keywords gets shredded: dependency/lineage analysis fabricates Read/Write edges to tables that the code never touches (proven: GHOST_TAB Write from a pure string assignment), and real statements can be silently dropped (proven: `v2 := 2` lost when a string contains the word BEGIN), corrupting the dep graph the product reports to users.
**Evidence:** stmt.rs:196-243 split_statements walks `let chars: Vec<char> = source.chars().collect();` matching consume_end_keyword (l208), consume_any_keyword(["BEGIN","IF","LOOP","CASE"]) (l217), and `if c == ';' && depth == 0` (l227) — NO single-quote or q-quote handling anywhere; strip_comments (l300) handles only `--`/`/* */`, no strings, and runs AFTER the split in lower_statement_body (l147). Sibling recover.rs:73-135 explicitly skips q-quotes and single-quoted literals (with `''` escape) before its BEGIN/END/`;` logic. Empirical run via real plsql_ir public API (cargo test -p plsql-ir, throwaway test, removed; tree confirmed pristine):
  CASE1 "v_sql := 'SELECT 1; SELECT 2';" -> [0] Assignment{target:"v_sql", rhs_text:"'SELECT 1"} [1] Sql{Select, "SELECT 2';"}
  CASE2 "v := 'BEGIN'; v2 := 2;" -> 1 stmt: Unrecognized{UnterminatedBlock} (v2:=2 LOST)
  CASE3 "EXECUTE IMMEDIATE 'INSERT INTO t VAL
**Repro/trigger:** Input X = a routine body statement `v_audit := 'old value; DELETE FROM ghost_tab';` (or any of CASE1-4). Trace: lower_statement_body -> split_statements splits at the in-string `;` (depth stays 0, no string skip) -> classify("DELETE FROM ghost_tab';") matches the DELETE verb -> Statement::Sql{Delete} -> extract_table_accesses -> accesses_from_sql(Delete) -> tables_after(...,"FROM") -> wrong output Y = TableAccess{table:"GHOST_TAB", access:Write}, a Write lineage edge to a table the code never executes against. CASE2 additionally yields Y = the entire body collapsing to one Unrecognized{UnterminatedBlock} with the trailing real statement lost. All reproduced live against plsql_ir::lower_state
**Suggested fix:** In split_statements, before the consume_end_keyword/consume_any_keyword/`;` checks in the while-loop, add literal-skipping that mirrors recover.rs:73-135: (1) single-quoted literals — on `'`, copy chars to buffer until the closing `'`, honoring the doubled-quote `''` escape, with no keyword/`;`/depth logic inside; (2) Oracle q-quote literals `q'X..X'` / `nq'..` recognized at a token boundary (prev char non-identifier), mapping bracket delimiters `[]{}()<>` and otherwise self-closing. strip_comments should likewise skip string literals so a `--`/`/* */` sequence inside a string is preserved. Add regression tests for CASE1-4 asserting: (a) `v_sql := 'SELECT 1; SELECT 2';` => exactly one Assignment with rhs `'SELECT 1; SELECT 2'`; (b) `v := 'BEGIN'; v2 := 2;` => two statements with v2:=2 preserved; (c) the multi-statement EXECUTE IMMEDIATE literal stays one ExecuteImmediate; (d) extract_tab

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: Made split_statements + strip_comments string-literal aware (and split_statements comment-aware, since it runs on the raw body BEFORE strip_comments). Added string_literal_end (single-quote with '' escape + Oracle q'X..X'/nq'X..X' alternative-quoting, token-start guarded, unterminated→EOF; mirrors plsql-parser-antlr::recover) and opaque_span_end (string OR line/block comment). split_statements copies opaque spans through verbatim so a ';' or BEGIN/END inside a literal/comment no longer splits or moves block depth; strip_comments skips literals so '--'/'/*' inside a string aren't stripped (corrupting SQL). 6 regression tests added (; in string/line-comment/block-comment, BEGIN/END in string, q-quote opacity, comment-markers-in-literal preserved). 261 plsql-ir + 94 plsql-sast green, clippy -D warnings clean. No cargo fmt (plsql-ir pre-existing).

title: plsql-cicd gate.rs: fix rustdoc reason names + validate blocking_unknown_reasons against known set
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-cicd/src/gate.rs:32 — CONFIRMED DEFECT, severity=medium
**Impact:** An operator who copies the rustdoc example writes `blocking_unknown_reasons = ["OpaqueDynamicSql", "DbLinkReference"]`; because the emitted names are `DynamicSqlOpaque`/`DbLinkRemoteObject`, the exact string match never fires and the deployment gate fails open silently — opaque dynamic SQL / db-link changesets pass the gate while the operator believes they are blocked.
**Evidence:** gate.rs:32 docstring (the only in-source example of allowed values): `//! blocking_unknown_reasons = ["OpaqueDynamicSql", "DbLinkReference"]`. The matcher at gate.rs:322-337 does `unknown_reason_name(&u.reason) == blocked_reason.as_str()`. unknown_reason_name() at gate.rs:369-370 emits `R::DynamicSqlOpaque => "DynamicSqlOpaque"` and `R::DbLinkRemoteObject => "DbLinkRemoteObject"`. Canonical enum plsql-core/src/lib.rs:212-213: `DynamicSqlOpaque, DbLinkRemoteObject` — no `OpaqueDynamicSql` or `DbLinkReference` variant exists. rg confirms `OpaqueDynamicSql` appears ONLY at gate.rs:32; `DbLinkReference` exists only as an unrelated struct (plsql-symbols/src/db_link.rs:33) and FactKind/FactPayload (plsql-ir/src/fact.rs:51,117), never as a reason matcher string. parse_policy() (gate.rs:134-136) is `toml::from_str(...).map_err(...)` with no value validation; `#[serde(default, deny_unknown_fields
**Repro/trigger:** Trigger: policy `blocking_unknown_reasons = ["OpaqueDynamicSql"]` (copied verbatim from gate.rs:32). Feed a prediction whose uncertainties contain `UnknownReason::DynamicSqlOpaque` (as predict.rs:471 actually emits). At gate.rs:327 unknown_reason_name returns "DynamicSqlOpaque" != "OpaqueDynamicSql" → filter().count()==0 → observed_count==0 → the `if observed_count > 0` guard at gate.rs:331 is false → no GateFailure::BlockingUnknownReasonHit pushed → allowed=true at gate.rs:342. Output Y: gate passes a changeset containing opaque dynamic SQL that the operator intended to block (silent fail-open). Disproof of any compile concern: this is a doc/string-value defect, not a build break; the match
**Suggested fix:** Correct the docstring at gate.rs:32 to `blocking_unknown_reasons = ["DynamicSqlOpaque", "DbLinkRemoteObject"]`. To eliminate the silent fail-open on a future typo, add value validation in parse_policy() (or run_gate): expose a `fn all_reason_names() -> &'static [&'static str]` derived from the same arms as unknown_reason_name(), and after parsing reject any entry of blocking_unknown_reasons not in that set with GateError::Parse. Add a test asserting every UnknownReason variant's unknown_reason_name() output is accepted and that "OpaqueDynamicSql" is rejected, so the docstring and matcher cannot drift silently again.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit b4c803fdbe), re-verified on main. Root-cause fixed two ways. (1) Corrected the only in-source example docstring at gate.rs:32 from the bogus ["OpaqueDynamicSql", "DbLinkReference"] to the actually-emitted ["DynamicSqlOpaque", "DbLinkRemoteObject"]. (2) Eliminated the silent fail-open on a future typo: parse_policy now validates every blocking_unknown_reasons entry against ALL_REASON_NAMES (the canonical UnknownReason variant names) and returns GateError::Parse for any unknown name, listing the valid set. ALL_REASON_NAMES is kept in lock-step with unknown_reason_name() by an exhaustive-match anti-drift test (all_reason_names_match_emitter) — a new UnknownReason variant becomes a compile/test failure rather than silent diverge

title: plsql-engine analyze_project: pair lowered declarations to bodies by span, not filtered index
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt + strict ground-truth calibration workflow (2026-06-02).

### Site crates/plsql-engine/src/lib.rs:915 — CONFIRMED DEFECT, severity=medium
**Impact:** In a deployment .sql file where a top-level DDL (e.g. CREATE TABLE) precedes two or more stored programs, the call-graph Calls edges and table Reads/Writes edges plus DependencyEdge facts of a later procedure/function are silently derived from an EARLIER object's body, corrupting find_callers/find_callees/what_breaks/plsql_analyze impact results with no diagnostic.
**Evidence:** lib.rs:903 `for (decl_idx, decl) in lowered.declarations.iter().enumerate()` then lib.rs:914-915 `ast.body_statements.get(decl_idx).filter(|s| !s.is_empty())`. ast.rs:432-435 documents body_statements is parallel with root.declarations (NOT lowered.declarations). tree_lower.rs:121-126 pushes to decls+body_stmts in lockstep; AstDecl::Ddl (line 239) and AstDecl::Unknown (line 246) each occupy a slot with an empty vec![] body. lower.rs:144-182: lower_top_level pushes NO declaration for AstDecl::Ddl/Unknown, only a diagnostic -> lowered.declarations is a filtered subset. analyze_project uses Antlr4RustBackend (lib.rs:788), the path that produces parallel body_statements. decl_idx is used only at lines 903 and 915 (grep confirmed), with no compensating span/index map. Throwaway integration test against the real APIs printed: root.declarations.len()=3, body_statements.len()=3 ([0]=len0 table, 
**Repro/trigger:** Input file: `CREATE TABLE foo (id NUMBER);` then `CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE p1 IS BEGIN INSERT INTO t1 VALUES(1); END; /` then `CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE p2 IS BEGIN INSERT INTO t2 VALUES(2); END; /`. Empirically (verified by running parse_with_backend(Antlr4RustBackend)+lower_top_level): caller P2 (lowered[1]) is positionally paired with body_statements[1] = P1's body, the non-empty filter passes, the correct span-text fallback is skipped, and P2 is attributed a Writes edge to T1 (P1's table) instead of T2. The common DDL-then-single-package case is masked only because the dropped DDL slot lands at empty index 0; the bug bites whenever a mis-indexed slot is non-empty (a real object prec
**Suggested fix:** Stop indexing parser-parallel ast.body_statements with the filtered decl_idx. Build a span-keyed map by zipping ast.root.declarations with ast.body_statements (each AstDecl exposes .span() via Spanned; each lowered Declaration carries decl.common().span), then in the loop look up body by decl.common().span instead of position. Add a regression test: CREATE TABLE followed by two procedures with distinct INSERT/CALL targets, asserting P2's Writes/Calls edges reference its own body (T2), not T1.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Root cause fixed in production code (not just the symptom).
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the defect before the fix (fails) and passes after.
- [ ] `cargo test -p <crate>` + `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt` (oraclemcp-* only).
- [ ] No behavior regression on existing tests / adversarial corpus.
close_reason: [done] implemented via parallel worktree workflow (commit efe4872204), re-verified on main. Root-cause fixed in analyze_project. The loop iterated the FILTERED lowered.declarations but indexed parser-parallel ast.body_statements with the lowered loop index decl_idx; a dropped DDL/Unknown decl ahead of real programs shifted indices, so a later proc's body (hence its Calls/Reads/Writes edges + DependencyEdge facts) was sourced from an earlier object. Fix builds a HashMap<Span,&Vec<AstStatement>> by zipping ast.root.declarations (via Spanned::span) with ast.body_statements, then looks up the body by decl.common().span (which lower_top_level threads through make_common, so it equals the AstDecl span) instead of by position. Text-scanner span-slice fallback preserved for decls whose spa
