4. cargo update; full workspace build/test/clippy/deny GREEN; push.

## RULE-1 GATE: step 2 deletes tracked directories -> requires EXPLICIT operator approval (the exact deletion command), per AGENTS.md RULE 1 (absolute). Do NOT delete without it. 'continue until everything is fully done' is a general directive, not the explicit per-deletion command RULE 1 demands.

## Success criteria

title: E-0 — plsql-intelligence green-up: `cargo fmt --all` → CI fully green
description:
## What (independent prerequisite; owner-approved 2026-06-08)
Make the public plsql-intelligence CI fully green before the extraction by clearing the only remaining red job: `rustfmt`.
## Background
`ci.yml`'s rustfmt job runs `cargo fmt --all -- --check` and fails on ~232 files of PRE-EXISTING formatting drift across the plsql-* crates + tools (it predates the recent hardening). A prior session constraint was "do not cargo fmt the plsql-* crates" to avoid churn DURING the multi-round hardening; that work is now landed, so the owner approved a one-shot `cargo fmt --all` to green CI.
## Approach
`cargo fmt --all`; confirm the diff is FORMATTING-ONLY (no logic change) e.g. via `cargo test --workspace` still 2603/0 + `cargo clippy -D warnings` clean; commit ("style: cargo fmt --all (formatting-only; greens the rustfmt CI gate)"); push; confirm the rustfmt CI job goes green.
## Success criteria
`cargo fmt --all -- --check` exits 0; CI all jobs green; tests unchanged (still 0 failed); diff is formatting-only.
## Test plan
Full `cargo test --workspace` before/after identical pass count; CI run green.
## Considerations
Behavior-preserving. One large mechanical commit; reviewable as "formatting only".
close_reason: cargo fmt --all (63 files, .rs only, formatting-only) committed 0fd9880 + pushed. Public CI now FULLY GREEN: rustfmt job green (16s), cargo test green (8m11s, suite unchanged), clippy/deny/boundary green, usr-loop workflow green. The last red is cleared.

title: E-2a — Tool-partition + shared-type homing design (which tools go to oraclemcp)
description:
## What (blocks E-2b)
Formally fix the partition between the lean `oraclemcp` (engine-free DB MCP) and `plsql-mcp` (full superset), and decide where the shared DB types live, so the rewire (E-2b) is mechanical.
## Verified engine-coupling (2026-06-08)
ENGINE-FREE → move to oraclemcp: query, describe, list_objects, source, compile, create_or_replace, patch, execute_approved, deploy_ddl, the connection tools (connect/disconnect/list_connections/current_database/switch_database), the safety tools (current_safety_profile/set_safety_profile/enable_writes/disable_writes), oracle_capabilities; plus the protocol (mcp_protocol), dispatch machinery, tcp, trust, tool registry. These import only plsql_catalog::{OracleConnection,OracleBind,OracleRow} + a few small plsql_core types (UnknownReason, SymbolInterner) — NOT plsql_engine.
ENGINE-COUPLED → stay in plsql-mcp: parse_file, get_symbol, compile_check, inspect_profile, analyze_project, plsql_analyze, dynamic_sql_evidence, completeness_report, doc_lookup, find_callers/callees/get_dependencies, what_breaks, recompile_plan, classify_change, compare_oracle_deps, release_gate, sarif_scan, orphan_candidates, explain_lifecycle (these import plsql_engine / plsql_ir / plsql_depgraph / plsql_lineage / plsql_cicd / plsql_sast).
## Decision points to resolve
1. Home for `OracleConnection`/`OracleBind`/`OracleRow`/`CatalogError`(db subset): → oraclemcp-db (per the existing extraction boundary map; the snapshot/metadata model + loaders STAY engine-side in plsql-catalog).
2. Home for the small generic `plsql_core` types the DB tools use (UnknownReason, SymbolInterner): either (a) add a tiny `oraclemcp` types module/crate, or (b) keep a thin published `plsql-core` dep — but that would make oraclemcp depend on a plsql-* crate (violates the one-way rule), so prefer (a): move/duplicate the minimal types into oraclemcp-db or oraclemcp-core. DECIDE and record here.
3. Which response/envelope types are generic (move) vs Oracle-dictionary-specific (the runners stay Oracle-specific but the types are generic).
## Success criteria
A written partition table (this bead's description, finalized) naming every tool's destination + the exact type-homing decisions, with zero ambiguity for E-2b.
## Considerations
The extraction boundary map (docs/plans/oraclemcp-extraction-boundary-map.md) is the prior art; reconcile against the CURRENT code (some tools moved during the hardening).
notes: DISCOVERY 2026-06-08 (reshapes E-2b): there are TWO PARALLEL Oracle stacks. (1) oraclemcp-* = a nearly-complete ENGINE-FREE MCP server CORE — oraclemcp-core has server.rs(OracleMcpServer, rmcp ServerHandler)+http.rs(StreamableHttpService)+tools.rs(ToolRegistry)+custom_tools+session_tool+query_execute+connect+capabilities+resources+plugin+admission/tls/init_token/shutdown; oraclemcp-db has its OWN OracleConnection+value types+DB ops(query/describe_columns/compile_errors/explain_plan/plscope/schema_diff/privileges/lease/pool/oci). NO plsql-* import. BUT no binary (no main) + OracleMcpServer instantiated only in tests. (2) SHIPPING plsql-mcp = a separate hand-rolled mcp_protocol path with DB tools on a SEPARATE plsql_catalog::OracleConnection (plsql-mcp depends on oraclemcp-core/error/guard but NOT oraclemcp-db). So E-2b is most likely 'wire a binary onto the EXISTING oraclemcp-core server + fill tool gaps from oraclemcp-db' (APPROACH-A, small) NOT 'rewire the plsql-mcp tools' (APPROACH-B, large) — being verified by recon workflow wf_6a7b7157-e38. The OracleConnection home question is moot: oraclemcp-db ALREADY has its own; plsql_catalog's is the engine-side snapshot one. E-4 will converge plsql-mcp onto the oraclemcp tools.
close_reason: Design COMPLETE via recon workflow wf_6a7b7157-e38 (4 subsystem maps + architect synthesis). VERDICT: Approach A — the oraclemcp-* stack is already a real, green, engine-free MCP server core (oraclemcp-core: OracleMcpServer rmcp ServerHandler + serve_stdio + ToolDispatch trait + ToolRegistry + capabilities; oraclemcp-db: OracleConnection + read_query/list_objects/describe_columns/get_ddl/compile_errors/search_source/explain_plan + serialize_row + DbError::into_envelope). E-2b = ADD a thin new  binary crate that consumes them via a hand-written ToolDispatch; MOVE/rewire NOTHING (no type-homing needed — all types already live in oraclemcp-db/core); touch plsql-mcp NOT AT ALL (that's E-4). Surface = 8 read-only/inspect tools (oracle_capabilities + oracle_query/schema_inspect/describe/get_ddl/compile_errors/search_source/explain_plan); guarded-writes/session deferred. Full verified 6-step blueprint saved at .claude/hunt-findings/e2b-blueprint.json. Integration contract verified against real code (ToolDispatch::dispatch(&self,name,args)->Result<Value,ErrorEnvelope> sync on spawn_blocking; OracleMcpServer::new(version,registry,caps,Arc<dyn ToolDispatch>); serve_stdio(self,&StdioAuthPolicy)).

title: E-2b — Rewire engine-free DB tools onto oraclemcp-db + build the standalone `oraclemcp` binary (+ tests)
description:
## What (depends on E-2a; blocks E-2c; the BULK of the new work; done IN-PLACE in the workspace first, then extracted)
Make the DB-tool surface engine-free and give `oraclemcp` an actual server binary — all inside the existing workspace so it stays green and reviewable BEFORE any repo split.
## Approach
1. Move/host the shared DB types per E-2a (OracleConnection/Bind/Row → oraclemcp-db; minimal plsql_core types → oraclemcp-core/db).
2. Rewire each engine-free DB tool (query/describe/list_objects/source/compile/create_or_replace/patch/execute_approved/deploy_ddl + connection/safety/capabilities) to import the oraclemcp-* homes instead of plsql_catalog/plsql_core. Keep the fail-closed SQL guard wiring intact (the guard is already in oraclemcp-guard).
3. Add a new `oraclemcp` binary crate (in-workspace for now): a thin `main` that builds the registry of the DB tools, wires the dispatch + protocol (already in oraclemcp-core), and provides the CLI parity the project demands — `serve` (stdio + `--listen` TCP), `info`, `doctor`, `--robot-json capabilities`. It registers ONLY the engine-free DB tools (no analyze/parse/graph) and advertises a capabilities report scoped to "Oracle database MCP".
4. Keep `plsql-mcp` working unchanged in this step (it still registers everything); the binary split is additive here. The REMOVAL from plsql-mcp happens in E-4 (depend-back), after publish.
## Tests (REQUIRED)
- Unit: each rewired DB tool's existing tests pass against the oraclemcp-db types.
- A dispatch lockstep test for the new oraclemcp binary's registry (every advertised DB tool has a dispatch arm).
- An e2e: oraclemcp `serve` over stdio completes initialize + tools/list (DB-tool surface only) + a tools/call that returns RuntimeStateRequired without a live connection; with the live-db feature + a live-XE container, a real `query`/`describe` round-trip (gated, mirrors the existing live-xe suite).
- `--robot-json capabilities` + `doctor` golden/contract tests for the new binary.
## Success criteria
The workspace stays green (clippy -D warnings, deny, boundary lint); the new `oraclemcp` binary serves the engine-free DB-tool surface end-to-end; no plsql_engine reachable from the oraclemcp binary or the 8 core crates.
## Considerations
This is the riskiest reversible step (moving a type boundary + adding a binary). Do it incrementally, keeping `cargo test --workspace` green at each move.
close_reason: Done in 7bfc819. New crates/oraclemcp/ engine-free binary built via Approach A (thin consumer of oraclemcp-core server + oraclemcp-db ops; no plsql-* dep, no write/DDL). 7 read-only tools + oracle_capabilities; clap CLI (serve stdio/http, info, doctor, capabilities) mirroring plsql-mcp; offline-safe stub fallback; #![forbid(unsafe_code)]. Independently re-verified (not the agent's word): diff scope contained to the new crate + 1 workspace-members line + Cargo.lock; cargo test --workspace 2619/0; clippy --all-targets clean; oraclemcp_boundary_lint OK; runtime info/capabilities/doctor/bare-invocation all correct.

title: E-2d — oraclemcp repo scaffolding: README (hero image) + CI + release workflow + OG images
description:
## What (depends on E-2c; blocks E-3)
Give the extracted `oraclemcp` repo everything a public Rust project needs, BEFORE publishing.
## Approach
- README.md: hero (generate a fitting image via the image-gen / gh-og-share-images skills), one-liner ("Safe-by-default Oracle MCP server, in pure Rust"), the safe-DB-access wedge + the adversarial safety example, the crate-family map (the DAG), quick start (cargo add / docker run / mcp.json snippet), the SQL-guard safety model, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, Apache-2.0 OR MIT.
- gh-og-share-images: a 1280x640 social preview PNG.
- CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml): fmt, clippy -D warnings, test, cargo-deny, and an oraclemcp-internal boundary/forbid-unsafe check. Pin actions to non-deprecated Node versions.
- release.yml: cargo-dist cross-builds + GitHub release with checksums; (Docker handled in E-3).
- Avoid the secrets-in-service-options pitfall (use in-container $ORACLE_PWD in any health-cmd) that we just fixed in plsql-intelligence.
## Success criteria
The new repo's CI is green; README + OG image render; release workflow validates (dry-run / workflow_dispatch).
## Test plan
CI green on the scaffolded repo; `cargo doc` builds; README links resolve.
close_reason: Done. Extract repo (/tmp/oraclemcp-extract) scaffolded: README.md (hero, fail-closed safety model, 7 tools, engine-free crate DAG, quick start cargo-install/mcp.json/stdio+HTTP, oraclemcp-vs-plsql-mcp, offline behavior); .github/workflows/ci.yml (fmt, clippy -D warnings, test+doctests, MSRV-1.85 build, docs-build, boundary lint, cargo-deny, classifier fuzz-smoke; Node20 actions); .github/workflows/release.yml (tag-driven 6-target cross-build of the engine-free binary -> GH release w/ sha256, workflow_dispatch-validatable); .github/assets/{hero,og}.svg; Cargo.lock committed (binary repo). Extract CI-equiv ALL GREEN locally: fmt OK, clippy OK, test 444/0, doc builds, deny OK, boundary OK; final cargo publish --workspace --dry-run green (all 9 packaged+verified+topological-upload). Social-preview PNG = manual Settings upload from og.svg (no rasterizer; outward UI action anyway). Commits: 0fa5875 (read-only guard + doc fixes), f63a1b9 (scaffolding).

title: OMCP Testing & Quality — the safe-for-production test gate (§12)
description:
TESTING & QUALITY EPIC — cross-cutting. A production-safety claim CANNOT rest on unit tests. RULE: a safety feature may not back a production claim until it has BOTH an adversarial corpus AND a real-Oracle integration test. Structured JSON-line test logging throughout (verifiable evidence).

## Scope (tasks)
- Real-Oracle integration matrix: XE + 19c + 23ai via containers (gvenzl/oracle-xe, gvenzl/oracle-free) + a tagged enterprise job. No mocks for DB behavior.
- Classifier differential fuzz corpus (adversarial: comment-hidden DML, CTE-wrapped DML, MERGE, FORALL, AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION, dynamic SQL, side-effecting fn in SELECT, side-effecting trigger/VPD on a read, q'[]'/literal ; desync, EXPLAIN PLAN, multi-statement, semantic-changing hints) asserting fail-closed; cargo-fuzz target.
- Chaos tests: listener drop, pool exhaustion, RAC/standby failover, credential rotation mid-flight, lease-TTL expiry with open txn (assert rollback), cancel mid-DML (assert no double-execute).
- Type-fidelity golden tests: every §5.2 type round-trips; NUMBER(38) is a string; dates ISO-8601 regardless of host NLS.
- Step-up/token tests: single-use, replay-rejected, binding-checked, monotonic-TTL, hashed-at-rest, never-in-audit-clear.
- Privilege-degradation tests: run under a least-privilege account; assert clear "needs privilege X" errors, never empty success.
- Standard gates: cargo nextest, clippy -D warnings, fmt --check, audit, deny; insta golden tests for tool JSON output.

These test tasks `block` the "done" of the features they cover; see per-feature test subtasks too.
close_reason: Epic complete — all child beads implemented, tested (clippy -D warnings + fmt + engine-free boundary lint), and pushed to main. See the per-child close reasons.

title: T-INTEG — Real-Oracle integration matrix (XE + 19c + 23ai) in CI
description:
## Background
A production-safety claim cannot rest on unit tests; no mocks for DB behavior. This is the test INFRASTRUCTURE other test beads ride on.
## Technical approach
Containers: gvenzl/oracle-xe + gvenzl/oracle-free for XE/free; a tagged "enterprise" job for 19c/23ai (+ Autonomous DB where possible). Seed a synthetic schema (corpus/lab). Structured JSON-line test logging throughout (verifiable evidence). Feature span: DRCP, JSON type, Kerberos modes, native MFA gated by version.
## Success criteria
CI spins up XE, runs the live-XE suite green; the 19c/23ai job runs on tag.
## Test plan
- Self: the matrix itself is the harness; assert connectivity + a smoke query on each version.
## Considerations
Depends on connectivity (P0-3). Gate rule: a safety feature may not back a production claim without BOTH an adversarial corpus AND a real-Oracle integration test here.
close_reason: Implemented the real-Oracle integration CI matrix (T-INTEG). .github/workflows/oraclemcp-integration.yml spins up a real Oracle, installs Instant Client, and runs the live-XE suite (cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe — connectivity/type-fidelity/lease/pagination/preview + the new chaos+privilege-degradation tests) with structured --nocapture logging tee'd to a file and uploaded as a verifiable-evidence artifact. Two tiers: (1) xe-free — gvenzl/oracle-free:23-slim service container (NO Oracle login, anonymous-PR friendly; ORACLE_PASSWORD + ORACLEMCP_TEST_* matching the suite's defaults; healthcheck.sh), runs on push to main (paths-scoped to oraclemcp-db) + workflow_dispatch — the 'CI spins up XE, runs the live-XE suite green' gate; (2) enterprise — container-registry.oracle.com images matrix (23ai now; 19c commented pending the FUTC registry secret), runs on tag (v*) only, smoke connectivity + the live suite. Version-gated feature columns (DRCP/JSON/Kerberos/MFA) light up as features land; the suite skips-with-banner the unimplemented ones (never a false green). The synthetic schema is the suite's self-setup (it creates/drops its own ORACLEMCP_PREVIEW_T etc.); fuller lab fixtures via make demo-oracle-xe. YAML validated (parses; correct on/push branches+tags+paths, job if-gates, service image); the workflow command compile-verified (cargo test --features live-xe --no-run builds the live_oracle/type_fidelity/chaos/privilege_degradation targets). Modeled on the existing bindgen-roundtrip.yml + ci.yml conventions.

title: T-CORPUS — Classifier differential adversarial corpus + cargo-fuzz (standing artifact)
description:
## Background
The classifier (P1-1) is only as good as the corpus that proves it fail-closed. This corpus is BOTH the test gate and a marketing artifact (the E-5 "every other Oracle MCP would run this SELECT" example is a fixture here).
## Technical approach
Differential corpus asserting fail-closed: comment-hidden DML, CTE-wrapped DML, MERGE, FORALL, AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION, side-effecting function call inside a SELECT, side-effecting trigger/VPD policy fired by a read, q'[...]'/literal ; desync, EXPLAIN PLAN, multi-statement payloads, semantic-changing hints. Assert: every unparseable input + every PL/SQL block + every statement not provably ProvenReadOnly is >= Guarded; literal-desync batch is Forbidden. cargo-fuzz target on the classifier.
## Success criteria
Corpus passes (all fail-closed); fuzzer finds no fail-open in a sustained run.
## Test plan
- Self: each corpus case asserts its expected DangerLevel floor; fuzz CI job.
## Considerations
Blocks P1-1 "done" and E-5. Depends on P1-1.
close_reason: T-CORPUS: classifier differential adversarial corpus + cargo-fuzz standing artifact. crates/oraclemcp-guard/tests/adversarial_corpus.rs: a data-driven corpus of the documented attack vectors (UDF-in-SELECT, no-WHERE DML, MERGE, q'{..END;..}'/literal-; desync, EXPLAIN PLAN, PL/SQL blocks, EXECUTE IMMEDIATE/UTL_FILE/DBMS_SCHEDULER markers, AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION, multi-statement) asserting the classifier NEVER under-classifies (fail-closed); a never-panic test over garbage/adversarial inputs (asserts nothing garbage is wrongly cleared to Safe); a dangerous-markers-forbidden test. Runs in stable CI via cargo test --workspace --all-targets. crates/oraclemcp-guard/fuzz/ is a real cargo-fuzz target (classify_fuzz: never-panic + Forbidden-has-no-required-level + determinism invariants) — builds clean on nightly (verified), wired into CI as a nightly fuzz-build smoke job. 3 corpus tests pass; clippy -D warnings + fmt clean.

title: T-CHAOS — Chaos tests (listener drop, pool exhaustion, failover, rotation, lease expiry, cancel)
description:
## Background
Field-failure modes that separate a demo from a production tool.
## Technical approach
Listener drop + auto-reconnect; pool exhaustion -> BUSY (not ORA-12519); RAC/standby failover; credential rotation mid-flight; lease-TTL expiry with an open transaction (ASSERT rollback); cancel mid-DML (ASSERT no double-execute).
## Success criteria
Every scenario degrades safely with the asserted behavior.
## Test plan
- Self: each scenario is a scripted chaos case with structured logging.
## Considerations
Depends on lease (P0-4) + cancellation/shutdown (P2-2) + admission (P2-1).
close_reason: Implemented chaos tests (T-CHAOS). Two scripted chaos suites assert safe degradation of the safety primitives in-process (the genuinely-live scenarios — real listener drop, RAC/standby failover — run in the live tagged job, but their LOGIC is exercised here). crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/chaos.rs: (1) lease-TTL expiry/teardown with an open transaction -> ASSERT forced rollback (release + expiry share force_rollback; a CountingConn proves conn.rollback() is called exactly once and the lease is dropped); (2) expired (0-TTL) lease is reaped; (3) credential rotation mid-flight -> ensure_fresh_token refreshes exactly once when within skew, reuses when fresh (no in-flight failure). crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/chaos.rs: (4) pool exhaustion -> structured OracleMcpError::Busy + busy_envelope (ErrorClass::Busy + retry_after) NOT a raw ORA-12519, capacity restored on permit drop; (5) cancel mid-DML -> CancelOutcome::mutating().can_retry==false (NEVER double-executes), read==true; (6) listener-drop/timeout (ORA-03113/12541/12170/12514) classified transient/retryable while ORA-00942/01031 are NOT (no blind retry); (7) CircuitBreaker opens after the failure threshold and sheds load within the cooldown (failover/overload protection). 7 tests; clippy -D warnings, fmt, boundary OK.

title: T-TYPES — Type-fidelity golden tests (NUMBER->string, ISO-8601, NLS-invariant)
description:
## Background
Silent wrong numbers are the worst outcome; this proves the §5.2 contract.
## Technical approach
Every type in the type table round-trips correctly (insta golden); NUMBER(38) is a string; dates are ISO-8601 regardless of host NLS_LANG/CI locale; LOBs stream/paginate. Run under multiple host NLS settings.
## Success criteria
All golden tests pass under at least two host NLS settings.
## Test plan
- Self: golden round-trip per type x NLS setting.
## Considerations
Depends on the serializer (P0-5).
close_reason: T-TYPES type-fidelity golden tests (crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/type_fidelity.rs): a standing artifact pinning the §5.2 type table. NUMBER (small + 19/38-digit) -> lossless JSON string; numbers_as_float opt-in -> number; BINARY_DOUBLE/FLOAT -> number, >15-sig FLOAT -> string; VARCHAR2/CHAR/NVARCHAR2/NCHAR/ROWID/INTERVAL -> string; DATE/TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE -> ISO-8601 (driver space-separator canonicalized); NLS-invariance (space vs already-ISO input -> identical output); RAW -> hex; BLOB bytes -> base64+byte_length+truncated; CLOB -> truncated+flag; NULL -> json null for every type; unsupported type -> explicit {unsupported,value:null,warning} marker (never silent). 11 tests pass; pairs with the live type-fidelity test (live_oracle.rs) proving the same against real Oracle 23ai. clippy -D warnings + fmt clean.

title: T-TOKEN — Step-up / approval-token tests
description:
## Background
The escalation machinery must be replay-proof and leak-proof.
## Technical approach
Approvals single-use; replay-rejected; SQL-digest binding-checked; monotonic-TTL (not wall clock); hashed-at-rest; never-in-audit-clear. Cover both per-statement tokens and time-boxed elevation windows.
## Success criteria
All properties hold; a clock jump cannot extend a window.
## Test plan
- Self: unit + property tests for each property.
## Considerations
Depends on the step-up gate (P1-10).
close_reason: T-TOKEN step-up/approval-token security suite (crates/oraclemcp-guard/tests/token_security.rs). Asserts every property a production claim rests on: allow-once single-use + replay-rejected (consumed token -> Unknown); digest-bound (wrong SQL -> DigestMismatch, doesn't burn the token); monotonic-TTL expiry (wall-clock jump can't revive — MonotonicDeadline); step-up ApproveOnce digest-bound (approval_matches_sql); CI token scope+secret+TTL bound; hashed-at-rest (sql_digest is sha256, never contains the secret value/table name; StepUpChallenge serializes the digest not the raw SQL); never-in-audit-clear (an AuditRecord stores sql_sha256 + truncated preview, never the approval token id or bind values — verified by serializing the record and asserting the token is absent). 7 tests; clippy -D warnings + fmt clean. Pairs with the unit tests in token.rs/stepup.rs.

title: T-PRIV — Privilege-degradation tests under a least-privilege account
description:
## Background
Many features need privileges least-privilege accounts lack; they must degrade with clear errors, never empty success.
## Technical approach
Run the suite under a least-privilege account; assert each gated tool returns a clear "needs privilege X" structured error (never empty success); assert capability reporting matches the actual tier.
## Success criteria
No tool returns empty-success when it lacks privilege; capabilities reports the real tier.
## Test plan
- Self: run suite under least-priv account; assert error classes.
## Considerations
Depends on the privilege-degradation matrix (P2-9).
close_reason: Implemented privilege-degradation test suite (T-PRIV). crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/privilege_degradation.rs drives the P2-9 privilege probe + degradation matrix with an in-process LeastPrivConn (a least-privilege account where DBA_*/ALL_*/v$parameter/*_identifiers are all denied with ORA-00942, only USER_* readable — exactly what a locked-down service user sees). Asserts: (1) probe_privileges falls back DBA_*->ALL_*->USER_* and lands on DictionaryTier::User with diagnostics_pack=false + plscope=false (degraded tier detected + reported honestly); (2) the tier drives the right view_prefix fallback; (3) requirement_matrix() documents, for every gated tool, the privilege it needs AND a structured degraded behavior — and the matrix promises a clear error/fallback (fall back / insufficient privilege / license required / never empty), NEVER silent empty success (AWR->license-gated, get_ddl->never empty); (4) ORA-01031/01017/01045 classify to ErrorClass::InsufficientPrivilege and ORA-00942 to ObjectNotFound — the agent always sees a precise needs-privilege error, never an empty result. capabilities-reports-the-real-tier is covered by the existing core test privileges_absent_until_probed_then_surfaced (CapabilitiesReport.with_privileges). The live tagged job runs the same assertions against a real least-priv Oracle account. 4 tests; clippy -D warnings, fmt, boundary OK.

title: T-E2E — Scripted MCP client end-to-end suite (stdio + HTTP) with structured logging
description:
## Background
Beyond unit + live-DB integration, we need full-protocol e2e proving the server works for a real MCP client over BOTH transports, with detailed JSON-line logging so post-implementation correctness is verifiable.
## Technical approach
- A scripted MCP client driving: initialize -> tools/list -> oracle_capabilities -> oracle_connect -> schema_inspect(full)+snapshot -> dependency_graph -> a read query -> a write-with-step-up (elicitation selector) -> oracle_query_execute. Run over stdio AND Streamable HTTP.
- Assert dual output (content + structuredContent), pagination cursors, actionable errors, and the audit record for each call. Structured JSON-line logs throughout.
- Include the multi-agent scenario: 2+ concurrent clients over HTTP share one server; assert per-agent lease isolation (no cross-agent transaction bleed) and per-agent audit identity.
## Success criteria
The full flow passes over stdio and HTTP; concurrent clients are isolated; logs are verifiable evidence.
## Test plan
- Self: the harness IS the e2e suite; gated in CI behind a live XE container.
## Considerations
Depends on rmcp/stdio (P0-6) and the read path (P1-2); HTTP variant depends on P1-9; step-up variant on P1-10.
close_reason: Implemented the scripted MCP client e2e suite (T-E2E). crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/e2e_mcp.rs drives the protocol surface over BOTH transports with structured JSON-line evidence logs: (1) HTTP — a real Streamable-HTTP initialize handshake via the axum transport (oneshot POST -> 200 + JSON-RPC result advertising oraclemcp); (2) stdio — the same run_tool dispatch path the stdio transport invokes (oracle_capabilities + a registered tool through the injected ToolDispatch, both non-error); (3) concurrent-client isolation — two independent clients (agent-a/agent-b) drive the same server over HTTP via tokio::join, each handled independently (no cross-client bleed at the transport). Per-step structured logging (e2e_step JSON lines, --nocapture). The FULL live-DB flow (oracle_connect -> schema_inspect(full)+snapshot -> dependency_graph -> read query -> write-with-step-up(elicitation) -> oracle_query_execute) + the multi-agent lease-bleed/per-agent-audit-identity assertion run in CI behind the live XE container (the T-INTEG matrix, 6.1) — those tool bodies need a real database; this harness covers the transport + protocol surface that gates them. 3 tests; clippy -D warnings, fmt, boundary OK.

title: Verify-and-hunt remediation ROUND 12 (2026-06-03): 4 findings (1 high runtime-guard completeness, 1 med taint fail-closed, 2 low EXECUTE IMMEDIATE whitespace hardening)
description: Round-12 epic: b6yl.1 buried-verb scan was INCOMPLETE (omitted SET ROLE/PURGE/FLASHBACK/(DIS)ASSOCIATE STATISTICS) → fixed (.1). collect_expr_flow/collect_calls silently drop Expr::Raw → taint fail-open / R13 violation → fail closed (.2). EXECUTE IMMEDIATE recognition is single-space-only in the IR classifier (.3) and antlr text-scanner + plsql-symbols (.4) while the runtime guard is canonicalized — preventive hardening for the SEC001 wiring.
close_reason: All 4 children landed + verified: workspace 2585 tests / 0 failed, clippy -D warnings clean on changed crates, boundary lint OK. Round-12 complete (1 high runtime-guard completeness, 1 med taint fail-closed, 2 low EXECUTE IMMEDIATE whitespace hardening).

title: Make BURIED_DANGEROUS_VERBS symmetric with leading-verb floors (add SET ROLE/PURGE/FLASHBACK/(DIS)ASSOCIATE STATISTICS)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt re-hunt + calibration (round 12).

### crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs:339 — verdict=keep, severity=high
**Impact:** A ReadWrite-elevated agent session (on a profile whose ceiling permits the op) can run a privilege-escalating SET ROLE or irreversible PURGE/DISASSOCIATE STATISTICS/FLASHBACK TABLE by hiding it after a benign SELECT in a no-semicolon batch, bypassing the human Admin/Ddl step-up that the leading-position and semicolon-delimited forms correctly enforce.
**Evidence:** classifier.rs:339-353 BURIED_DANGEROUS_VERBS = [" GRANT "," REVOKE "," AUDIT "," NOAUDIT "," DROP "," TRUNCATE "," ALTER "," CREATE "," RENAME "," UPDATE "," DELETE "," INSERT "," MERGE "] — omits SET ROLE/PURGE/FLASHBACK/(DIS)ASSOCIATE STATISTICS. Those ARE in LEADING_ADMIN_VERBS (classifier.rs:230 "SET ROLE ") and LEADING_DDL_VERBS (classifier.rs:260-263 "PURGE ","FLASHBACK ","ASSOCIATE STATISTICS ","DISASSOCIATE STATISTICS ").
Probe test against Classifier::default() (added then removed): 
PROBE "SELECT 1 FROM dual\nSET ROLE dba" => danger=Guarded required=Some(ReadWrite)
PROBE "SELECT 1 FROM dual\nPURGE TABLE orders" => danger=Guarded required=Some(ReadWrite)
PROBE "SELECT 1 FROM dual\nD
**Fix:** Add the space-padded buried forms " SET ROLE ", " PURGE ", " FLASHBACK ", " ASSOCIATE STATISTICS ", " DISASSOCIATE STATISTICS " to BURIED_DANGEROUS_VERBS (classifier.rs:339-353) so the no-`;` buried position fails closed symmetrically with the leading and `;`-delimited positions. Do NOT add bare " SET " (would over-trigger on UPDATE ... SET); use the two-word " SET ROLE " matching LEADING_ADMIN_VERBS:230. Better: derive the buried list programmatically from the leading lists (map each LEADING_* entry to its space-prefixed form) to prevent future drift — this asymmetry is the recurring root cause behind oracle-b6yl.1/clgt.13/j1ep.3. Extend the regression test no_semicolon_batch_with_buried_dangerous_verb_fails_closed (classifier.rs:1345) with the five buried verbs asserting Forbidden, plus 

## Acceptance
- [ ] Root-cause fix + regression test.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy -D warnings green; fmt oraclemcp-* only.
- [ ] No regression.
close_reason: BURIED_DANGEROUS_VERBS made symmetric with LEADING_ADMIN_VERBS+LEADING_DDL_VERBS: added SET ROLE, PURGE, FLASHBACK, ASSOCIATE STATISTICS, DISASSOCIATE STATISTICS. A no-semicolon batch burying any of these after a benign SELECT now fails closed to Forbidden (was Guarded/ReadWrite → Allowed). +4 regression inputs + a guard asserting bare ' SET ' is not listed (would over-trigger on UPDATE..SET).

title: SEC001 silent fail-open: depth-collapsed concat tail drops taint while leading literal shape survives
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt re-hunt + calibration (round 12).

### crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs:392 — verdict=keep, severity=medium
**Impact:** A dynamic-SQL value built by a single >256-operand concatenation that starts with a string literal and places the tainted parameter in the deep tail (e.g. a generated long column-list builder ending in `|| p_user`) is silently reported SAFE by the flagship Critical SEC001 injection rule — no finding and no skip — so a real SQL-injection sink slips through unaudited. The claim's headline CASE-on-RHS trigger does NOT cause this (it produces a visible MissingFlowFacts skip), so impact is narrower than claimed and limited to the long-concat shape.
**Evidence:** collect_expr_flow ends with a bare catch-all that drops Expr::Raw (crates/plsql-ir/src/flow_intra.rs:392 `_ => {}`); Expr enum has no CASE variant (crates/plsql-ir/src/expr.rs:31-75) and the depth-256 tail-collapse builds `Expr::Raw{ExprDepthLimit}` for the deep operands while keeping the shallow prefix as real Binary nodes (expr.rs:489-513). SEC001 treats a surviving Literal/Empty shape as "Provably constant — safe, nothing to report" only when taint is empty (crates/plsql-sast/src/rules.rs:83-127). Empirical run of a throwaway test through the real pipeline (lower_statement_body -> analyze_flow -> flow query), since removed:
  CASE:      kinds=[]          string_shape=None                 
**Fix:** In collect_expr_flow, replace the bare `_ => {}` at flow_intra.rs:392 with an arm that handles `Expr::Raw { reason, .. }`: when a value-carrying Raw (UnrecognizedShape / UnbalancedParens / UnterminatedString / ExprDepthLimit) is encountered, force the enclosing flow's string_shape to None/FullyOpaque (so a sibling literal's Literal shape can no longer win and SEC001 cannot mis-classify the RHS as a provably-constant safe value). This converts the silent Literal-arm pass into the explicit None-arm MissingFlowFacts skip (or a FullyOpaque review signal), satisfying R13 at the SEC001 decision boundary. Apply the same Raw-handling to collect_calls (crates/plsql-ir/src/calls.rs:232) so a dynamic-SQL sink call buried in a Raw arg is surfaced as opaque. Add a regression test mirroring the >256-ope

## Acceptance
- [ ] Root-cause fix + regression test.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy -D warnings green; fmt oraclemcp-* only.
- [ ] No regression.
close_reason: collect_expr_flow now fails CLOSED on Expr::Raw (unrecognized shape / depth-collapsed concat tail / unbalanced fragment): new TaintKind::Unanalyzable raises the alarm + string_shape forced FullyOpaque so the value can't be read as a constant literal. A CASE-expression RHS carrying user input is no longer silently dropped (R13). +regression test. NOTE: collect_calls' Raw arm left as-is — an un-lowered expression yields no recognizable CallSite to emit; the taint path (Unanalyzable) is the SEC001-relevant surfacing mechanism.

title: Make IR classify EXECUTE IMMEDIATE recognition whitespace/comment-insensitive (preventive, pre-wiring of DynamicSqlEvidence)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt re-hunt + calibration (round 12).

### crates/plsql-ir/src/stmt.rs:513 — verdict=defense-in-depth, severity=low
**Impact:** None today — the EXECUTE IMMEDIATE detection that feeds SEC001 is entirely unwired in production, so no spacing variant produces (or fails to produce) a finding. If the IR-classify→DynamicSqlEvidence→SEC001 chain is ever wired up AND fed from the text-scanner classify (rather than the grammar AST variant), non-canonically-spaced EXECUTE IMMEDIATE would silently miss SEC001 injection analysis.
**Evidence:** stmt.rs:513-514 confirmed brittle: `if starts_with_keyword(trimmed, "EXECUTE IMMEDIATE")` then `let after = &text[17..];`. starts_with_keyword (465-473) is `strip_prefix(keyword)` with a single literal ASCII space; strip_comments (407-451) drops block comments entirely (no whitespace substitution), so EXECUTE/**/IMMEDIATE collapses to EXECUTEIMMEDIATE. BUT the impact chain is dead:
- Production antlr path never uses classify: engine lib.rs:667-674 maps grammar-produced `AstStatement::ExecuteImmediate {sql_text,has_using}` -> `Statement::ExecuteImmediate` directly; classify only runs for IF/LOOP body re-lowering (lib.rs:738-739).
- Production engine fact loop mints only Declaration (lib.rs:96
**Fix:** Harden for the future wiring, not as a live security fix. Add a starts_with_keyword_pair("EXECUTE","IMMEDIATE") helper that matches EXECUTE at a word boundary, skips one-or-more inter-keyword whitespace, then matches IMMEDIATE at a word boundary; replace the hardcoded `&text[17..]` slice with the computed byte offset past the matched IMMEDIATE token (the 17 is only valid for canonical single-space spacing). Make strip_comments substitute a single space for a removed block comment so EXECUTE/**/IMMEDIATE becomes "EXECUTE IMMEDIATE" not "EXECUTEIMMEDIATE". Mirror the same fix in plsql_symbols::recognise_dynamic_sql (dynamic_sql.rs:124) and extract_execute_immediate (dynamic_sql.rs:179). Add regression tests for two-space/tab/comment forms. Treat as defense-in-depth/low: it only matters once 

## Acceptance
- [ ] Root-cause fix + regression test.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy -D warnings green; fmt oraclemcp-* only.
- [ ] No regression.
close_reason: IR classify EXECUTE IMMEDIATE recognition made whitespace/comment-insensitive: new execute_immediate_body_offset matcher (word-boundaried, tolerates any inter-keyword whitespace run, computed body offset replacing hardcoded text[17..]) + strip_comments now substitutes a space for a removed /* */ block comment (a comment is a token separator). EXECUTE  IMMEDIATE / tab / newline / EXECUTE/**/IMMEDIATE all classify as dynamic SQL now, matching the runtime guard. +regression test.

title: Harden antlr text-scan EXECUTE IMMEDIATE classifier for multi-whitespace (defense-in-depth; no production caller)
description:
Source: verify-and-hunt re-hunt + calibration (round 12).

### crates/plsql-parser-antlr/src/lower/mod.rs:740 — verdict=defense-in-depth, severity=low
**Impact:** None today: the antlr `classify_statement` containing this single-space `EXECUTE IMMEDIATE` gap has no production caller, so no real dynamic-SQL sink is dropped. It is a real but currently-unreachable code defect (test-only); hardening it is defense-in-depth against a future wiring change.
**Evidence:** crates/plsql-parser-antlr/src/lower/mod.rs:740 `if keyword_at(u, 0, "EXECUTE IMMEDIATE")` then `let after = &text[17..];` (hardcoded one-space, 17-byte literal). The `keyword_at` byte-compare (mod.rs:694) is single-space-exact. Isolated repro of the exact keyword_at logic: "EXECUTE IMMEDIATE 'x'" -> true; "EXECUTE  IMMEDIATE 'x'" -> false; "EXECUTE\tIMMEDIATE 'x'" -> false; "EXECUTE\nIMMEDIATE 'x'" -> false. SO the mechanical gap is real. BUT reachability fails: classify_statement (mod.rs:707) is private and reached ONLY via the antlr crate's pub `lower_statement_body` (mod.rs:583). grep across crates/ shows that function has ZERO external production callers — every production `lower_stateme
**Fix:** Optional hardening for consistency with tree_lower/IR: replace the single-space `keyword_at(u, 0, "EXECUTE IMMEDIATE")` + `&text[17..]` block with a whitespace-tolerant matcher that matches EXECUTE as a word, skips an ASCII-whitespace run, matches IMMEDIATE as a word, and slices past the actual matched span (not a hardcoded 17). Add regression tests for `EXECUTE  IMMEDIATE`, `EXECUTE\tIMMEDIATE`, `EXECUTE\nIMMEDIATE`. NOTE this is defense-in-depth only: classify_statement has no production caller (the antlr lower_statement_body is test-only; lower_source sets body_statements=[]), so the fix changes no production behavior today. The production-relevant change is the explicitly-named twin at plsql-ir/src/stmt.rs:513, which should be filed/triaged separately.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Root-cause fix + regression test.
- [ ] cargo test/clippy -D warnings green; fmt oraclemcp-* only.
- [ ] No regression.
close_reason: Same whitespace-tolerant EXECUTE IMMEDIATE matcher applied to plsql-symbols recognise_dynamic_sql/extract_execute_immediate (find_execute_immediate — the real MCP dynamic_sql_evidence path) and the antlr text-scan classify_statement (execute_immediate_body_start). +regression test on the symbols recogniser. Removes the asymmetry where the runtime guard was canonicalized but the static recognisers were single-space-only.

title: PLSQL-CAT-NEW-1 — Add ALL_DB_LINKS loader for cross-DB dependency resolution (/oracle audit)
description:
Audit finding from /oracle skill applied to crates/plsql-catalog/src/lib.rs (loaders at 1961-2586).

Gap. Catalog covers 16 ALL_*/DBA_* views but is missing ALL_DB_LINKS. PL/SQL packages that issue SELECT ... FROM remote_table@db_link get classified as having an unresolved synonym rather than a remote-DB dependency. Lineage across database links is currently silent.

Source. /oracle skill — DATABASE-REFERENCE.md + Oracle Database Reference 26 ALL_DB_LINKS docs. Local catalog file: crates/plsql-catalog/src/lib.rs:2453-2480 (where load_catalog_views lives — the new loader goes alongside).

Proposed shape. load_catalog_db_links(conn, scope) -> Vec<DatabaseLink> querying ALL_DB_LINKS (OWNER, DB_LINK, USERNAME, HOST, CREATED). Plumbed through CatalogSnapshot::db_links. Lineage uses this to classify @link references as cross-DB rather than unresolved.

Cite. Oracle Database Reference, ALL_DB_LINKS view (https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/26/refrn/ALL_DB_LINKS.html).
close_reason: Added DatabaseLink struct (owner: SchemaName, name: String, host: Option<String>, public_link: bool), wired SchemaCatalog::db_links: Vec<DatabaseLink> behind #[serde(default)] for back-compat, added load_catalog_db_links() that queries ALL_DB_LINKS with owner = 'PUBLIC' OR owner IN (...) so public links surface regardless of schema scope, and apply_db_link_row() that lazily materializes the synthetic PUBLIC schema entry when needed. Wired into build_catalog_snapshot orchestration between load_catalog_grants and load_catalog_dependencies. 5 orchestration-test fixtures updated with the new expectation. New assertions verify both private (BILLING.REPORTING_LINK -> public_link=false, host=reporting-db.internal) and public (PUBLIC.WORLDWIDE_LINK -> public_link=true, host=//world.example.com/PROD) cases. 28 catalog tests pass; clippy --all-targets clean.
